title: Lettres de cachet
content:
Lettres de cachet (;) were letters signed by the king of France, countersigned by one of his ministers, and closed with the royal seal. They contained orders directly from the king, often to enforce arbitrary actions and judgments that could not be appeal ed. In the case of organized bodies,'lettres de cachet’ were issued for the purpose of preventing assembly or accomplishing some other definite act.

The provincial estates were convoked (called to assembly) in this manner, and it was by a "lettre de cachet" (in this case, a "lettre de jussipri"), or by showing in person in a "lit de justice", that the king ordered a "parlement" to register a law despite that "parlement"s refusal to pass it.
